payload.text: I’ll inspect the repo structure and the existing ticket creation/draft paths first, then implement the smallest schema and UI/backend changes that satisfy the ticket without changing unrelated behavior.

I’ve found a compact Electron/React codebase: shared types, a Markdown storage service, main-process IPC, and one large renderer component. I’m reading those surfaces now so the epic model stays consistent across all layers.

{
  "name": "relay",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Local-first Codex-driven kanban desktop app.",
  "main": "./out/main/index.js",
  "type": "module",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "electron-vite dev",
    "dev:logs": "electron-vite dev 2>&1 | tee /tmp/relay-dev.log",
    "build": "tsc --noEmit && electron-vite build",
    "logs": "mkdir -p \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay\" && touch \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay/relay.log\" && tail -n 200 -f \"$HOME/Library/Application Support/relay/relay.log\"",
    "logs:dev": "touch /tmp/relay-dev.log && tail -n 200 -f /tmp/relay-dev.log",
    "preview": "electron-vite preview",
    "test": "node tests/run-tests.mjs",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@dnd-kit/core": "^6.3.1",
    "@dnd-kit/sortable": "^10.0.0",
    "@dnd-kit/utilities": "^3.2.2",
    "@openai/codex-sdk": "^0.130.0",
    "clsx": "^2.1.1",
    "gray-matter": "^4.0.3",
    "lucide-react": "^0.468.0",
    "react": "^19.0.0",
    "react-dom": "^19.0.0",
    "ulid": "^2.3.0",
    "zod": "^3.24.1"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "^22.10.2",
    "@types/react": "^19.0.2",
    "@types/react-dom": "^19.0.2",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^4.3.4",
    "electron": "^33.2.1",
    "electron-vite": "^2.3.0",
    "typescript": "^5.7.2",
    "vite": "^5.4.11"
  }
}

The current create modal is Codex-first, so I’m going to extend it rather than introduce a separate flow: a ticket type selector and manual fields will create task/epic tickets directly, while the Codex draft path will use the selected type and show generated subtickets before saving. On the backend I’m storing both `parentEpicId` and ordered `subticketIds` so the relationship is readable and stable after reload.

{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "useDefineForClassFields": true,
    "lib": ["ES2022", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"],
    "allowJs": false,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true,
    "strict": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleResolution": "Bundler",
    "baseUrl": ".",
    "paths": {
      "@shared/*": ["src/shared/*"],
      "@renderer/*": ["src/renderer/src/*"]
    },
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "types": ["node", "vite/client"]
  },
  "include": ["electron.vite.config.ts", "src", "tests"],
  "references": []
}
I’m starting with shared contracts because every other layer depends on them. This adds a backward-compatible `task` default plus explicit epic relationship fields and IPC input shapes.

Next I’m updating validation. Older Markdown tickets that do not have epic metadata will parse as normal task tickets, while malformed draft output with nested or task-owned subtickets will fail validation.

labels: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
  context: z.string().default(""),
  researchFindings: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
  requirements: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
  implementationPlan: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
  acceptanceCriteria: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
  clarificationQuestions: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
  implementationNotes: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
  research: ticketDraftResearchSchema.default({
    generatedAt: "",
    checkedUrls: [],
    inspectedFiles: [],
    limitations: [],
    limits: {
      maxResearchMs: 0,
      maxUrls: 0,
      maxUrlFetchMs: 0,
      maxUrlContentChars: 0,
      maxFilesToScan: 0,
      maxFilesToRead: 0,
      maxFileReadChars: 0,
      maxMatchesPerFile: 0
    }
  })
});
*/

export const agentTicketUpdateSchema = z
  .object({
    title: z.string().min(1),
    priority: z.enum(["low", "medium", "high", "urgent"]),
    labels: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
    markdown: z.string().min(1),
    clarificationQuestions: z.array(z.string()).default([])
  })
  .strict();

export const clarificationQuestionSchema = z
  .object({
    id: z.string().min(1),
    ticketId: z.string().min(1),
    question: z.string().min(1),
    answerType: z.literal("text"),
    answer: z.string().nullable().default(null),
    createdAt: isoString,
    updatedAt: isoString,
    answeredAt: isoString.nullable().default(null),
    createdBy: z.enum(["user", "codex", "system"]),
    source: z.enum(["manual_board", "manual_ticket_edit", "agent_execution", "clarification_ui", "system_reconciliation"]),
    runId: z.string().nullable().default(null),
    codexThreadId: z.string().nullable().default(null)
  })
  .passthrough();

export const clarificationStoreSchema = z
  .object({
    schemaVersion: z.literal(1),
    ticketId: z.string().min(1),
    questions: z.array(clarificationQuestionSchema).default([])
  })
  .passthrough();
I’m moving into storage now. The key parts are relationship validation, ordered parent lists, child back-references, and cleanup on unlink/delete so subtickets stay independent board tickets.

Storage now supports creating epics with generated subtickets, linking existing tickets, unlinking without deletion, and clearing child references when an epic is deleted. I’m wiring those operations through IPC next.
I’m updating the Codex draft contract now. The model can still return the old single-ticket shape, but it now has a strict epic shape with normal task subtickets and no nested epics.

The renderer now has the main interaction points: type selection during creation, manual save without Codex, epic draft review with child tickets, and an epic detail section for creating, linking, opening, and unlinking subtickets. I’m adding the corresponding styles so those controls stay compact and consistent with the existing UI.
The first implementation pass is in place. I’m running the type checker now to catch contract mismatches before adding and adjusting tests.
TypeScript passes. I’m adding focused coverage for the new draft contract and backend persistence/reload behavior, including backward-compatible tickets with no `ticketType` metadata.
The tests now exercise epic draft conversion, relationship persistence, unlink/delete behavior, nested-epic rejection, and legacy ticket defaults. I’m running typecheck and the full test suite next.
Both typecheck and the full test suite pass. I’m doing a final diff review for accidental churn and any obvious UX or relationship edge cases before I wrap up.

Implemented epic mode end to end.

**Summary**
- Added `task`/`epic` ticket typing plus `parentEpicId` and ordered `subticketIds` front matter.
- Added backend create/link/unlink subticket operations, including nested-epic/self-link validation and cleanup when subtickets or epics are deleted.
- Updated create UI with ticket type selection, manual ticket creation, epic draft review, and generated subticket review.
- Added epic detail UI for parent/child navigation, creating subtickets, linking existing task tickets, and unlinking without deletion.
- Updated Codex ticket drafting schema/prompt to support epic drafts with normal task subtickets.
- Added regression coverage for epic drafts, persisted relationships, reload behavior, independent child status moves, unlink/delete behavior, nested epic rejection, and legacy tickets without epic metadata.

payload.finalResponse:
Implemented epic mode end to end.

**Summary**
- Added `task`/`epic` ticket typing plus `parentEpicId` and ordered `subticketIds` front matter.
- Added backend create/link/unlink subticket operations, including nested-epic/self-link validation and cleanup when subtickets or epics are deleted.
- Updated create UI with ticket type selection, manual ticket creation, epic draft review, and generated subticket review.
- Added epic detail UI for parent/child navigation, creating subtickets, linking existing task tickets, and unlinking without deletion.
- Updated Codex ticket drafting schema/prompt to support epic drafts with normal task subtickets.
- Added regression coverage for epic drafts, persisted relationships, reload behavior, independent child status moves, unlink/delete behavior, nested epic rejection, and legacy tickets without epic metadata.

**Files Changed**
- `src/shared/types.ts`
- `src/main/services/schemas.ts`
- `src/main/services/storage.ts`
